result:
Good — `redis_fsync` is a plain syscall macro, no additional shared state. `reclaimFilePageCache` in util.c just calls `posix_fadvise`-style syscalls on the fd, no globals. So the shared-state surface is fully contained in bio.c/server.h fields. Here's the full list:

## Shared state touched by `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` (bg thread) that main thread also touches

| State | Bg thread access | Main thread access | Synchronization |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`bio_jobs[worker]`** (per-worker job list) | `listFirst`/`listDelNode` (bio.c:290,365) | `listAddNodeTail` in `bioSubmitJob` (bio.c:185); length check in `bioDrainWorker` | `bio_mutex[worker]` — held around every access |
| **`bio_jobs_counter[BIO_NUM_OPS]`** | `bio_jobs_counter[job_type]--` (bio.c:366), done while holding lock | `bio_jobs_counter[type]++` in `bioSubmitJob` (bio.c:186); read in `bioPendingJobsOfType` (bio.c:376) | `bio_mutex[worker]` (same per-worker mutex, consistently held for both list and counter) |
| **`bio_comp_list`** | `listAddNodeTail` (bio.c:351) when a completion job runs | swapped out / drained in `bioPipeReadJobCompList` (bio.c:428-432, runs on main/event-loop thread) | `bio_mutex_comp` |
| **`job_comp_pipe[1]`/`job_comp_pipe[0]`** | bg thread `write()`s a byte (bio.c:354) | main thread `read()`s in the ae file-event handler `bioPipeReadJobCompList` (bio.c:424) | No mutex — relies on the pipe itself (self-pipe trick) as the synchronization/wake-up primitive; it only signals "check `bio_comp_list`", it carries no payload, so atomicity of the 1-byte write/read is sufficient |
| **`server.aof_bio_fsync_status`** (`redisAtomic int`, server.h:2309) | `atomicSet` on fsync failure/success (bio.c:321,328) | `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` in aof.c (flushAppendOnlyFile, etc.) and server.c (cron, INFO) | C11/GCC atomic ops via the `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` macros — no mutex, lock-free atomics |
| **`server.aof_bio_fsync_errno`** (`redisAtomic int`, server.h:2310) | `atomicSet` on fsync failure (bio.c:322) | `atomicGet` in server.c:5220 | Same — `redisAtomic` macros |
| **`server.fsynced_reploff_pending`** (`redisAtomic long long`, server.h:2376) | `atomicSet` after successful fsync (bio.c:329) | `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` throughout aof.c and server.c (tracking replication offset progress) | Same — `redisAtomic` macros |
| **`server.bio_cpulist`** | read once at thread start via `redisSetCpuAffinity(server.bio_cpulist)` (bio.c:267) | set from config at startup (config.c:3230, `createStringConfig(..., IMMUTABLE_CONFIG, ...)`) | None needed — it's an `IMMUTABLE_CONFIG`, so it's only ever written during config load, which happens-before `bioInit()`/thread creation; no runtime write-side race is possible |

## Explicitly not shared / not racy

- **`bio_mutex[]`, `bio_newjob_cond[]`, `bio_mutex_comp`** — these are the synchronization primitives themselves, not data.
- **`bio_threads[]`** — written once by main thread in `bioInit`, later only read by main thread in `bioKillThreads`; the bg thread never touches it, so no cross-thread access exists.
- **`bio_job_to_worker[]`, `bio_worker_title[]`** — `static` read-only lookup tables, never written after program init.
- Each `bio_job *job` payload itself — allocated by main thread, handed to the bg thread via the mutex-protected list, freed by the bg thread (`zfree(job)`, bio.c:360); ownership transfer is covered by the `bio_jobs[worker]` mutex above, so there's no separate race on the job memory.
